ModuleName: Data Visualization Principles and Excel Charting

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's learning topics—covering chart selection, visual hierarchy, color use, and avoiding misleading representations—used by instructors to organize content and by learners to navigate what will be taught.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: An overview page listing and briefly describing all topics covered in the module — including visualization foundations, chart selection, design principles, misleading representations, and Excel charting — used by learners and instructors to navigate the module's contents.

Artifacts.Name: Data_Visualization_Principle_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing data visualization principles and Excel charting, covering foundational concepts such as choosing the right chart type, visual hierarchy, and avoiding misleading representations, used by instructors to deliver lessons and by learners to review key concepts and design guidelines.

Artifacts.Name: Foundations of Data Visualization
Artifacts.Description: A reading that introduces data visualization as a discipline, explaining why encoding data into visual forms reveals patterns that raw numbers obscure, covering core principles of effective visual communication, and used by learners as foundational context before exploring specific chart types or tools.

Artifacts.Name: Choosing the Right Chart Type
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explains how to match chart types (bar, line, pie, scatter, etc.) to specific data relationships and analytical goals, helping learners make principled, evidence-based decisions about which chart to use rather than defaulting to aesthetic preferences.

Artifacts.Name: Visual Hierarchy and Design Principles
Artifacts.Description: A reading that covers how visual design elements — size, color, position, and weight — create hierarchy in charts, guiding the viewer's eye toward the most important information, used by learners to apply intentional design choices that improve clarity and comprehension.

Artifacts.Name: Avoiding Misleading Representations
Artifacts.Description: A reading that examines how charts can distort or misrepresent data through manipulated axes, misleading scaling, selective framing, and other deceptive techniques, teaching learners to recognize and avoid both accidental and deliberate visualization errors.

Artifacts.Name: Creating Charts in Excel
Artifacts.Description: A reading that walks learners through Excel's charting tools and workflow, covering how to insert charts, select and map data, choose chart types, and fine-tune formatting to produce clear and accurate visual representations of spreadsheet data.

Artifacts.Name: Building Effective Visual Summaries in Excel
Artifacts.Description: A reading that goes beyond basic chart insertion to cover how to design cohesive, audience-ready visual summaries in Excel by combining chart choices, layout, labeling, and formatting decisions that together communicate a clear and immediate data story.

Artifacts.Name: Building Effective Visual Summaries in Excel
Artifacts.Description: A reading that goes beyond basic chart insertion to cover how to design cohesive, audience-ready visual summaries in Excel by combining chart choices, layout, labeling, and formatting decisions that together communicate a clear and immediate data story.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question bank covering topics such as the purpose of data visualization, appropriate chart selection, Tufte's data-ink ratio, misleading y-axis truncation, and pie chart usage, used by instructors to build quizzes and assessments that test learners' understanding of visualization principles.
